evals.name: score skill prompt quality
evals.prompt:
A user asks: review and score this skill prompt for quality. Prompt:
---
name: helper
description: Helps with development stuff.
allowed-tools: [Bash, Read, Write]
---
# Helper
You are a brilliant coding assistant. Explore the repo, improve whatever seems useful, and make the code better. Explain your reasoning and write files when needed.
---
Describe the review workflow and report scores.
evals.expected_output: The response scores the provided prompt across 8 dimensions (signal density, scope specificity, output structure, format efficiency, failure handling, grounding discipline, routing precision, progressive disclosure), reports concrete issues, and proposes fixes.
assertions: The output scores signal density or mentions token efficiency or fluff.
The output scores scope specificity or notes missing scope boundaries.
The output scores output structure or notes missing output format.
The output scores format efficiency or checks for tables/diagrams/italic.
The output scores failure handling or notes missing failure cases.
The output scores grounding discipline or notes missing evidence requirements.
The output scores routing precision or checks trigger quality in the description.
The output scores progressive disclosure or checks instruction length.
The output reports actionable findings with concrete fixes.

evals.name: reject generic style review
evals.prompt: A user asks: make this agent prompt sound nicer and more polished. Describe how the reviewing-instructions skill should respond.
evals.expected_output: The response avoids cosmetic tone editing as the main goal and instead reviews instruction quality across behavioral dimensions.
assertions: The output says the primary goal is not cosmetic polish or generic style improvement.
The output redirects to instruction quality criteria such as triggers, scope, tools, safety, or verifiability.
The output asks for or uses the actual prompt content before making findings.
The output avoids rewriting for tone without identifying behavioral issues.
The output produces findings that can be verified or tested.
